title: Seed K-ORC bootstrap clouds.yaml from operator
description:
# GitHub Issue #412: feat(c5c3-operator): seed the K-ORC bootstrap clouds.yaml from the operator to drop the ControlPlane-name coupling

**Description:**

K-ORC (the OpenStack Resource Controller) needs a working password-based `clouds.yaml` to authenticate the *first* admin application-credential mint — the credential-based `clouds.yaml` does not exist until the c5c3-operator mints it, so the very first authentication is a chicken-and-egg problem. Today that first `clouds.yaml` is **pre-seeded by deploy-infra tooling before any `ControlPlane` CR exists**, and the seed has to guess the projected Keystone Service name (`{name}-keystone`) up front to build the in-cluster `auth_url`. This couples the OpenBao bootstrap shell to the CR name through two knobs (`CONTROLPLANE_NAME`, `KORC_KEYSTONE_AUTH_URL`) and a static, single-identity ExternalSecret. This issue moves the seed into the c5c3-operator, which already computes the projected name at reconcile time, so no name has to be known ahead of time.

The codebase already contains almost all the machinery needed; this issue closes the last gap. The boundaries below pair what already exists against what this issue adds.

**Concrete boundary 1 — the projected auth_url already lives in the operator.**
`keystoneEndpointURL(cp)` in `operators/c5c3/internal/controller/reconcile_korc.go:1242` returns exactly `http://{keystoneName(cp)}.{childNamespace(cp)}.svc:5000/v3`, where `keystoneName(cp)` = `cp.Name + "-keystone"` (`reconcile_keystone.go:41`, suffix at `reconcile_keystone.go:29`) and `childNamespace(cp)` = `cp.Namespace` (`reconcile_infrastructure.go:43`). For the default CR this is `http://controlplane-keystone.openstack.svc:5000/v3` — byte-identical to the URL the shell seed derives. **This issue adds** nothing new for the URL: the seed the operator writes reuses this function, so the operator-owned seed is automatically correct for any CR name.

**Concrete boundary 2 — the operator already builds the password-based clouds.yaml.**
`buildPasswordCloudsYAML(cp, password)` (`reconcile_korc.go:250`) renders the exact `clouds:` document the shell seed writes, including the critical `endpoint_type: internal` key (NOT `interface`) and the in-cluster `auth_url`. It is already called by `ensureAdminPasswordCloud` (`reconcile_korc.go:221`) to populate the `{cp.Name}-admin-password-cloud` Secret the ApplicationCredential mints against. **This issue adds** a second use of `buildPasswordCloudsYAML`: seeding the bootstrap `clouds.yaml` key so K-ORC's admin imports and the AdminCredential gate can proceed without a shell seed. The `endpoint_type: internal` triage consideration is satisfied for free by reusing this function.

**Concrete boundary 3 — the per-CR OpenBao path and the PushSecret already exist.**
`adminAppCredentialRemoteKeyFor(cp)` (`reconcile_korc.go:61`) returns `openstack/keystone/{cp.Namespace}/{cp.Name}/admin/app-credential`, and `adminAppCredentialPushSecret(cp)` (`reconcile_korc.go:1074`) mirrors the `{cp.Name}-admin-app-credential` Secret's `clouds.yaml` key (`appCredCloudsYAMLKey` = `"clouds.yaml"`, `reconcile_korc.go:126`) to that path via `secrets.EnsurePushSecret`. Today that PushSecret is created only inside `reconcileAdminCredential` (`reconcile_korc.go:1004-1005`), *after* the mint. **This issue adds** an earlier, ungated creation of the same PushSecret in `reconcileKORC`, sourced from a password-based `clouds.yaml` written into the same Secret key, so the path is populated before the credential exists.

**Concrete boundary 4 — the gate is a deliberate chicken-and-egg that the shell seed breaks.**
`reconcileAdminCredential` gates the credential push on the `k-orc-clouds-yaml` ExternalSecret being Ready in `childNamespace(cp)` (`reconcile_korc.go:886-908`), but that ExternalSecret materializes *from* the OpenBao path the push *writes*. On a fresh cluster the path is empty, so the gate never opens and the push never runs — a permanent deadlock unless something seeds the path first. The unit test `TestReconcileAdminCredential_FreshClusterWithoutCloudsYamlSeedDoesNotPush` (`reconcile_korc_test.go:383`) *asserts this exact deadlock holds* ("no PushSecret may be created while the clouds.yaml ExternalSecret is unseeded"). **This issue adds** the operator-owned seed that breaks the cycle and **reworks that test** to assert the operator now seeds and the chain converges.

**Concrete boundary 5 — the seed is currently shell, keyed on names known up front.**
`seed_korc_bootstrap_clouds_yaml()` (`deploy/openbao/bootstrap/write-bootstrap-secrets.sh:158-218`) writes the password `clouds.yaml` to `kv-v2/openstack/keystone/${ns}/${cp}/admin/app-credential` with `auth_url=${KORC_KEYSTONE_AUTH_URL:-http://${keystone}.${ns}.svc:5000/v3}`, called from `main()` at line 274 per `KORC_CONTROLPLANES` identity, followed by `mark_eso_managed "${ac_path}"` (line 278). `hack/deploy-infra.sh:1198-1201` exports `KORC_CONTROLPLANES="openstack/${CONTROLPLANE_NAME}"` and `KORC_KEYSTONE_AUTH_URL="http://${CONTROLPLANE_NAME}-keystone.openstack.svc:5000/v3"` under `WITH_CONTROLPLANE=true`. **This issue removes** the `seed_korc_bootstrap_clouds_yaml` definition + call, the now-unused `ac_path` local and its `mark_eso_managed` call, the `KORC_KEYSTONE_AUTH_URL` knob entirely (it has no other consumer — confirmed by grep), and the `KORC_KEYSTONE_AUTH_URL` export in deploy-infra.

**Concrete boundary 6 — the static ExternalSecret is a single-identity shim that names this issue.**
`deploy/eso/externalsecrets/k-orc-clouds-yaml.yaml` defines two ExternalSecrets (namespaces `openstack` and `orc-system`), both reading the hardcoded key `openstack/keystone/openstack/controlplane/admin/app-credential`. Its own comment (lines 24-28) says: *"This static, single-identity remoteRef.key is a deployment shim: https://github.com/.../issues/412 supersedes it with operator-owned per-CR templating ... Until then, bringing up a non-default ControlPlane requires editing this key (and CONTROLPLANE_NAME in `make deploy-infra`) in lockstep."* For an arbitrary CR name the hardcoded key does not match `adminAppCredentialRemoteKeyFor(cp)`. **This issue adds** an operator-created, owner-referenced, per-CR `k-orc-clouds-yaml` ExternalSecret in `childNamespace(cp)` (reading `adminAppCredentialRemoteKeyFor(cp)`), and **removes the `openstack`-namespace document** from the static manifest to avoid two controllers owning the same object. The `orc-system` document is kept static (see boundary 7).

**Concrete boundary 7 — the `orc-system` copy is off the AdminCredential critical path.**
K-ORC resolves each resource's `CloudCredentialsRef` in the resource's *own* namespace, so the credential critical path (`AdminCredentialReady`) only needs the `childNamespace(cp)` copy — confirmed by the gate at `reconcile_korc.go:886-887` and the co-location note in the manifest (lines 30-41). The `orc-system` copy serves K-ORC's "global default mount" and the e2e verify job (`tests/e2e/c5c3/full-controlplane-keystone/01-openstack-verify-job.yaml`, which runs in `orc-system`). Cross-namespace owner references are disallowed (a ControlPlane in namespace `openstack` cannot own an object in `orc-system`), so the operator cannot own it. **This issue keeps** the `orc-system` document static (default-identity only) and documents it as the remaining off-critical-path shim. The "arbitrary CR name" acceptance criterion targets `AdminCredentialReady`, which the per-CR `childNamespace` copy fully satisfies.

**Concrete boundary 8 — the operator already has the RBAC and condition wiring.**
The kubebuilder marker `controlplane_controller.go:82` already grants `external-secrets.io` `externalsecrets;pushsecrets` `create;update;patch;delete`, and the Helm ClusterRole mirrors it (`operators/c5c3/helm/c5c3-operator/templates/_helpers.tpl:195-200`). The instrumentation map `instrumentation.go:40-44` already maps the `KORC` and `AdminCredential` sub-reconcilers to their condition types. **This issue adds** no new RBAC group/resource and no new sub-reconciler or status condition, so neither the RBAC-sync pattern nor the condition-coverage drift guard is triggered.

**Concrete boundary 9 — the idempotency guard reuses an existing key check.**
The acceptance requirement "never overwrites a minted credential-based `clouds.yaml`" maps to a write-if-empty guard on the `appCredCloudsYAMLKey` of the `{cp.Name}-admin-app-credential` Secret: the operator writes the password seed only when that key is absent/empty. After the mint, `reconcileAdminCredential` fills it with the credential version (`reconcile_korc.go:984-986`), so the seed step becomes a no-op on every subsequent pass. On a re-mint, `regenerateAppCredentialSecretValue` deletes that key (`reconcile_korc.go:657`), so the seed gracefully repopulates the password version and bridges the re-authentication gap until the new credential is assembled. This mirrors the existing "write if missing" discipline of the shell seed (`write-bootstrap-secrets.sh:196-199`) and the `Kubernetes Operator Reconciliation` idempotency pattern.

### Recommended implementation (so the plan is executable)

Keep the OpenBao+ESO approach (it is the existing architecture, materializes both namespaces from one push, and keeps step 3 of the proposal unchanged). Do NOT switch to a direct in-cluster Secret write — it would conflict with the ESO-owned `k-orc-clouds-yaml` Secret (`creationPolicy: Owner`) and would not cover the `orc-system` copy the verify job reads.

In `reconcileKORC` (`reconcile_korc.go:306`), after `ensureAdminPasswordCloud` and `ensureAppCredentialSecret` succeed and **before** `ensureKORCAdminImports` (`reconcile_korc.go:383`), add three steps:

1. `seedBootstrapCloudsYAML(ctx, cp, password)` — `CreateOrUpdate` the `{cp.Name}-admin-app-credential` Secret; if `secret.Data[appCredCloudsYAMLKey]` is empty, set it to `[]byte(buildPasswordCloudsYAML(cp, password))`; leave the `value` key untouched; set the controller reference. (Write-if-empty = boundary 9.)
2. `secrets.EnsurePushSecret(ctx, r.Client, r.Scheme, cp, adminAppCredentialPushSecret(cp))` — ensure the `{cp.Name}-admin-app-credential-backup` PushSecret exists now, not only after the mint. It is already clobber-safe/idempotent (DeepEqual guard inside `EnsurePushSecret`), so the later call in `reconcileAdminCredential` is a harmless no-op.
3. `ensureKORCCloudsYAMLExternalSecret(ctx, cp)` — `CreateOrUpdate` an `external-secrets.io/v1` `ExternalSecret` named `cp.Spec.KORC.AdminCredential.CloudCredentialsRef.SecretName` (defaulted to `k-orc-clouds-yaml`, `controlplane_webhook.go:34`) in `childNamespace(cp)`, owner-referenced to `cp`, `secretStoreRef` `{Kind: ClusterSecretStore, Name: openBaoClusterStoreName}`, `target.name` the same Secret name with `creationPolicy: Owner`, and one `data` entry `{secretKey: "clouds.yaml", remoteRef: {key: adminAppCredentialRemoteKeyFor(cp), property: "clouds.yaml"}}`.

No surrounding logic in `ensureKORCAdminImports`, the mint, or `reconcileAdminCredential` changes; `importCredRef` keeps using `k-orc-clouds-yaml` (now operator-seeded). K-ORC's admin Domain/User CRs are created immediately and K-ORC retries authentication asynchronously until ESO materializes the Secret, so no explicit wait is needed.

**Motivation:**

Bringing up a `ControlPlane` with any name other than `controlplane` today requires editing three coupled artifacts in lockstep — `CONTROLPLANE_NAME` / `KORC_KEYSTONE_AUTH_URL` in `make deploy-infra`, and the hardcoded `remoteRef.key` in the static `k-orc-clouds-yaml` ExternalSecret — and the seed runs in bootstrap shell that has no way to learn the projected Keystone name. The operator already computes that name at reconcile time. Keeping the seed in shell means the operator and tooling must agree on a naming convention forever, and the `multi-controlplane` e2e fixtures (`tenant-a/controlplane-a`, `tests/e2e/c5c3/multi-controlplane/00-tenant-a-controlplane.yaml`) cannot work on the default `deploy-infra` path at all.

The decoupling also removes a fragile bootstrap ordering. The current flow depends on a human (or `WITH_CONTROLPLANE_CR`) keeping the applied CR name equal to the seed identity; a mismatch yields a silent failure mode the issue body documents — K-ORC resolves the unreachable external Gateway endpoint, swallows the connection error, and reports the admin Domain/User imports as empty ("Waiting for OpenStack resource to be created externally") with no credential ever minted. Centralizing the seed in the operator, which derives both the path and the `auth_url` from the same `ControlPlane` object, makes that mismatch structurally impossible.

This is the last shim between the c5c3-operator and a fully self-contained ControlPlane bootstrap. Boundaries 1-3 show the operator already owns the password `clouds.yaml`, the OpenBao path, and the PushSecret; only the *first* write and the per-CR ExternalSecret are still external. Closing that gap unblocks arbitrary-name and multi-tenant ControlPlanes on the default deploy path and lets the static-shim comment in `deploy/eso/externalsecrets/k-orc-clouds-yaml.yaml` (which names this issue) be deleted.

**Affected Areas:**

- operators/c5c3/internal/controller/reconcile_korc.go — add `seedBootstrapCloudsYAML(ctx, cp, password)` (write-if-empty password `clouds.yaml` into the `{cp.Name}-admin-app-credential` Secret's `clouds.yaml` key) and `ensureKORCCloudsYAMLExternalSecret(ctx, cp)` (per-CR, owner-referenced ExternalSecret in `childNamespace(cp)`); call both plus `secrets.EnsurePushSecret(...adminAppCredentialPushSecret(cp))` inside `reconcileKORC` before `ensureKORCAdminImports`. No changes to `importCredRef`, the mint, or `reconcileAdminCredential` beyond the idempotent PushSecret call already there.
- operators/c5c3/internal/controller/reconcile_korc_test.go — rework `TestReconcileAdminCredential_FreshClusterWithoutCloudsYamlSeedDoesNotPush` (it asserts the old deadlock); add tests: seed writes the password `clouds.yaml` when the key is empty; seed does NOT overwrite a non-empty (minted) `clouds.yaml`; `reconcileKORC` creates the per-CR ExternalSecret with the right `remoteRef.key` (= `adminAppCredentialRemoteKeyFor(cp)`), namespace (= `childNamespace(cp)`), `secretKey`/`property` (= `clouds.yaml`), and a controller owner reference; the seeded `clouds.yaml` parses with `endpoint_type: internal` and the projected `auth_url`. Reuse helpers `korcControlPlane()`, `korcTestScheme(t)`, `setKORCReady(cp)`, `mintedAppCredSecret(cp)`, `readyCloudsYamlES(cp)`.
- operators/c5c3/internal/controller/integration_test.go — the brownfield/managed integration tests pre-create the `k-orc-clouds-yaml` ExternalSecret to simulate the shell seed (lines 305-315); change them to assert the operator creates it instead, then `SimulateExternalSecretSync` against the operator-created object.
- deploy/openbao/bootstrap/write-bootstrap-secrets.sh — delete `seed_korc_bootstrap_clouds_yaml()` (lines 158-218), its call (line 274), the now-unused `ac_path` local (line 255) and its `mark_eso_managed "${ac_path}"` (line 278); keep the Model B admin-password seed (lines 261-266) and its `mark_eso_managed "${admin_path}"`. Update the `KORC_CONTROLPLANES` header comment (lines 23-34) to stop describing the AC-clouds.yaml seed.
- hack/deploy-infra.sh — delete the `KORC_KEYSTONE_AUTH_URL` export (line 1200); keep the `KORC_CONTROLPLANES` export (line 1199 — still needed for the Model B admin-password path). Update the surrounding comment block (lines 1170-1197) and the parenthetical at lines 1188-1190 that points at the now-removed coupling. Update the `WITH_CONTROLPLANE_CR` user guidance (lines 1320-1321) that tells users to keep `CONTROLPLANE_NAME` in lockstep with the K-ORC seed.
- deploy/eso/externalsecrets/k-orc-clouds-yaml.yaml — remove the `openstack`-namespace ExternalSecret document (lines 44-65) now owned by the operator; keep the `orc-system` document (lines 66-85) as the off-critical-path default-identity copy; rewrite the header comment to delete the "issue #412 supersedes it" shim language and describe the new operator-owned/static split.
- deploy/eso/kustomization.yaml — `k-orc-clouds-yaml.yaml` (line 18) stays (it still ships the `orc-system` document).
- tests/e2e/c5c3/full-controlplane-keystone/chainsaw-test.yaml — the link-by-link assertions and diagnostics reference the seed and the `orc-system` ExternalSecret (lines 227-305); confirm the default-name run still passes end-to-end with the operator-owned seed (it should, unchanged path key); update any comment that attributes the seed to `write-bootstrap-secrets.sh`.
- tests/e2e/c5c3/multi-controlplane/ — this suite (`controlplane-a`/`tenant-a`, `controlplane-b`/`tenant-b`) becomes runnable on the default deploy path; verify the per-CR ExternalSecret materializes for the non-default names and `AdminCredentialReady=True` is reached without per-identity seed overrides.
- docs/quick-start-controlplane.md, docs/reference/c5c3/controlplane-reconciler.md, docs/reference/c5c3/controlplane-crd.md, docs/reference/infrastructure/infrastructure-manifests.md — update prose that describes the bootstrap `clouds.yaml` as deploy-infra-seeded, the `CONTROLPLANE_NAME`/`KORC_KEYSTONE_AUTH_URL` lockstep requirement, and the static `k-orc-clouds-yaml` ExternalSecret; describe the operator-owned seed and per-CR ExternalSecret instead.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

- [ ] On a fresh kind cluster with `WITH_CONTROLPLANE=true`, a `ControlPlane` with an arbitrary name and namespace reaches `AdminCredentialReady=True` **without** `write-bootstrap-secrets.sh` running `seed_korc_bootstrap_clouds_yaml` (the function no longer exists) — verified by the e2e `full-controlplane-keystone` suite plus a non-default-name run (e.g. the `multi-controlplane` fixtures).
- [ ] `grep -rn KORC_KEYSTONE_AUTH_URL` over the repo returns no matches in `hack/` or `deploy/openbao/bootstrap/` (the knob is fully removed); `make deploy-infra WITH_CONTROLPLANE=true` succeeds with no `KORC_KEYSTONE_AUTH_URL` set.
- [ ] `reconcileKORC` creates a `k-orc-clouds-yaml` ExternalSecret in `childNamespace(cp)`, owner-referenced to the `ControlPlane`, whose single `data` entry has `remoteRef.key == adminAppCredentialRemoteKeyFor(cp)`, `secretKey == "clouds.yaml"`, and `property == "clouds.yaml"` — asserted by a new unit test.
- [ ] The seeded `clouds.yaml` (from `buildPasswordCloudsYAML`) contains `endpoint_type: internal` and `auth_url: http://{cp.Name}-keystone.{cp.Namespace}.svc:5000/v3` — asserted by a unit test that parses the rendered document, not just the input.
- [ ] Re-running `reconcileKORC` after the credential is minted leaves the `{cp.Name}-admin-app-credential` Secret's `clouds.yaml` key holding the credential-based document (the seed never overwrites it) — asserted by a unit test that sets a non-empty credential `clouds.yaml`, runs the seed, and checks the bytes are unchanged.
- [ ] The reworked `TestReconcileAdminCredential_FreshClusterWithoutCloudsYamlSeedDoesNotPush` (or its replacement) asserts the new behavior: with the operator seed present, the PushSecret IS created and the OpenBao path IS written; the old "no push on a fresh cluster" assertion is gone.
- [ ] `go test ./operators/c5c3/...` passes, including the updated `integration_test.go` that asserts the operator creates the `k-orc-clouds-yaml` ExternalSecret rather than pre-creating it.
- [ ] `deploy/eso/externalsecrets/k-orc-clouds-yaml.yaml` no longer contains an `openstack`-namespace ExternalSecret (only the `orc-system` one), and `kubectl apply -k deploy/eso/` applies cleanly with no duplicate-owner conflict against the operator-created object.
- [ ] E2E `full-controlplane-keystone` passes with the new flow (the issue's named gate).

**Non-Goals:**

- **Removing `CONTROLPLANE_NAME` entirely.** It remains required for the Model B admin-password seed (`bootstrap/{ns}/{keystone}/admin`, `write-bootstrap-secrets.sh:261-266`) and for the bundled-CR rename under `WITH_CONTROLPLANE_CR` (`hack/deploy-infra.sh:1285-1289`). Those are the keystone-operator password-bootstrap mechanism, not the K-ORC `clouds.yaml`; decoupling them is a separate concern.
- **Making the `orc-system` `k-orc-clouds-yaml` copy per-CR.** Cross-namespace owner references are disallowed, and that copy is off the `AdminCredentialReady` critical path (boundary 7). It stays a static default-identity manifest for the global mount and the e2e verify job.
- **Changing the AC's mint credential (`importCredRef` / `acCredRef`) or the re-mint logic.** The imports keep authenticating via `k-orc-clouds-yaml` and the AC keeps minting via `{cp.Name}-admin-password-cloud`; the integration test that "locks in that split" (`integration_test.go:415-426`) is unchanged. This issue only changes *who writes the first seed*.
- **Touching the standalone (per-service) Keystone path.** K-ORC seeding only runs on the ControlPlane stack; the standalone keystone deployment does not seed a K-ORC `clouds.yaml`, so nothing there changes.
- **Adding new RBAC, CRD fields, or status conditions.** The operator already has `externalsecrets;pushsecrets` RBAC (kubebuilder marker + Helm mirror) and the `KORC`/`AdminCredential` condition wiring; no `*_types.go` change means no `make manifests`/`make generate` regeneration is in scope.

**References:**

- `operators/c5c3/internal/controller/reconcile_korc.go` — `reconcileKORC` (306), `reconcileAdminCredential` (860), `buildPasswordCloudsYAML` (250), `ensureAdminPasswordCloud` (221), `ensureAppCredentialSecret` (134), `adminAppCredentialRemoteKeyFor` (61), `adminAppCredentialPushSecret` (1074), `keystoneEndpointURL` (1242), `appCredCloudsYAMLKey` (126), `korcCloudsYamlSecretName` (87), `regenerateAppCredentialSecretValue` (641).
- `operators/c5c3/internal/controller/reconcile_keystone.go:41` (`keystoneName`), `reconcile_infrastructure.go:43` (`childNamespace`), `controlplane_controller.go:82` (ESO RBAC marker) and `:103-131` (sub-reconciler order), `instrumentation.go:40-44` (condition map).
- `operators/c5c3/api/v1alpha1/controlplane_webhook.go:34` (`DefaultCloudCredentialsSecretName`), `:112-116` (one-ControlPlane-per-namespace).
- `operators/c5c3/internal/controller/reconcile_korc_test.go:383` (the deadlock test to rework) and helpers at `:1015`, `:1029`, `:1065`; `integration_test.go:305-315` (ExternalSecret pre-create to flip).
- `deploy/openbao/bootstrap/write-bootstrap-secrets.sh:158-218,274,278` (seed to remove); `hack/deploy-infra.sh:102,1198-1201,1320-1321` (knobs to drop/update); `deploy/eso/externalsecrets/k-orc-clouds-yaml.yaml:24-28,44-85` (static shim that names this issue); `deploy/eso/kustomization.yaml:18`.
- `deploy/kind/controlplane/controlplane.yaml` (default CR), `tests/e2e/c5c3/full-controlplane-keystone/` and `tests/e2e/c5c3/multi-controlplane/` (e2e suites), `operators/c5c3/helm/c5c3-operator/templates/_helpers.tpl:195-200` (Helm RBAC mirror).
- Review patterns grounding this elaboration: **Kubernetes Operator Reconciliation** (idempotent create-or-update; write-if-empty seed never clobbers the minted credential), **Kubernetes Secrets Management** / External Secrets (OpenBao+ESO over direct Secret writes), **GitOps Principles** (operator owns the per-CR ExternalSecret; the static `orc-system` copy stays declarative), **Kubernetes Server-Side Apply / field ownership** (remove the `openstack`-namespace static ExternalSecret so two controllers never own one object), and the project patterns *Hardcoded sub-resource names prevent multi-instance support* and *Sync RBAC markers with Helm ClusterRole templates* (no new RBAC, so the markers and `_helpers.tpl` already match).
- Related: #403 (shared `commonv1` types), #411 (default well-known database/cache/secret fields), prior work CC-0110 (ControlPlane reconciler), CC-0112 (per-ControlPlane credential scoping).

---

_Elaborated by [planwerk-review](https://github.com/planwerk/planwerk-review) with Claude Code_


**Labels:** enhancement

---
Source: https://github.com/C5C3/forge/issues/412

stories.title: Platform operator brings up an arbitrarily-named ControlPlane on the default deploy path
stories.want: to apply a ControlPlane CR with any name/namespace and have it reach AdminCredentialReady=True on a fresh cluster without editing any K-ORC seed knobs
stories.so_that: I am not forced to keep CONTROLPLANE_NAME, KORC_KEYSTONE_AUTH_URL and a hardcoded ExternalSecret remoteRef.key in lockstep
criteria: Applying a ControlPlane named other than 'controlplane' reaches AdminCredentialReady=True without running seed_korc_bootstrap_clouds_yaml (the function no longer exists)
No KORC_KEYSTONE_AUTH_URL value needs to be exported for make deploy-infra WITH_CONTROLPLANE=true to succeed
The operator creates a k-orc-clouds-yaml ExternalSecret in the ControlPlane's own namespace whose remoteRef.key equals openstack/keystone/{cp.Namespace}/{cp.Name}/admin/app-credential
No manual 'kubectl annotate externalsecret force-sync' workaround is required for the chain to converge

stories.title: The c5c3-operator self-seeds the bootstrap clouds.yaml and per-CR ExternalSecret
stories.want: to write a password-based clouds.yaml into the per-CR OpenBao path and create the matching ExternalSecret during reconcileKORC, before the credential is minted
stories.so_that: the AdminCredentialReady chicken-and-egg gate opens on a fresh cluster without any external shell seed
criteria: During reconcileKORC, seedBootstrapCloudsYAML writes buildPasswordCloudsYAML(cp, password) into the {cp.Name}-admin-app-credential Secret's clouds.yaml key when that key is empty
During reconcileKORC, secrets.EnsurePushSecret is called with adminAppCredentialPushSecret(cp) so the {cp.Name}-admin-app-credential-backup PushSecret exists before the mint
During reconcileKORC, ensureKORCCloudsYAMLExternalSecret create-or-updates an esov1.ExternalSecret owner-referenced to the ControlPlane in childNamespace(cp)
All three steps run after ensureAppCredentialSecret and before the re-mint/CreateOrUpdate AC decision, on every pass

stories.title: The operator never clobbers a minted credential-based clouds.yaml
stories.want: the bootstrap seed to be write-if-empty so it becomes a no-op once K-ORC has minted and reconcileAdminCredential has filled the credential-based clouds.yaml
stories.so_that: the minted application-credential document is never overwritten by the password bootstrap on subsequent reconciles
criteria: When the {cp.Name}-admin-app-credential Secret's clouds.yaml key is non-empty, seedBootstrapCloudsYAML leaves the bytes unchanged
After reconcileAdminCredential fills the credential-based clouds.yaml (buildAppCredCloudsYAML), a subsequent reconcileKORC pass does not modify it
The 'value' key is never touched by the seed
On a re-mint (regenerateAppCredentialSecretValue deletes the clouds.yaml key), the next reconcileKORC pass repopulates the password-based clouds.yaml

stories.title: The seeded clouds.yaml authenticates K-ORC in-cluster
stories.want: the operator-seeded clouds.yaml to carry endpoint_type: internal and the projected in-cluster Keystone auth_url
stories.so_that: K-ORC resolves the cluster-DNS identity endpoint and does not silently fail against the unreachable external Gateway endpoint
criteria: The rendered clouds.yaml, when parsed as YAML, has clouds.<cloud>.endpoint_type == 'internal' (the key is endpoint_type, NOT interface)
The rendered clouds.yaml auth_url == http://{cp.Name}-keystone.{cp.Namespace}.svc:5000/v3
auth_type/identity_api_version match buildPasswordCloudsYAML output (v3 password auth)
The assertion parses the rendered document, not just the format-string input

stories.title: Deploy-infra maintainer no longer maintains the K-ORC auth_url lockstep
stories.want: the KORC_KEYSTONE_AUTH_URL knob and the shell seed function removed, with KORC_CONTROLPLANES retained only for the Model B admin-password seed
stories.so_that: there is one fewer naming convention the operator and tooling must agree on forever
criteria: grep -rn KORC_KEYSTONE_AUTH_URL over hack/ and deploy/openbao/bootstrap/ returns no matches
seed_korc_bootstrap_clouds_yaml(), its call site, the ac_path local and its mark_eso_managed call are deleted from write-bootstrap-secrets.sh
The Model B admin-password seed (write_secret_if_missing on bootstrap/{ns}/{keystone}/admin + its mark_eso_managed) is retained unchanged
KORC_CONTROLPLANES export in deploy-infra.sh is retained (still drives the Model B loop); KORC_KEYSTONE_AUTH_URL export is deleted

stories.title: GitOps maintainer sees one operator-owned ExternalSecret and one static off-path copy
stories.want: the static k-orc-clouds-yaml manifest reduced to only the orc-system document, with the openstack-namespace document removed
stories.so_that: two controllers never own the same object and kubectl apply -k deploy/eso/ stays clean
criteria: deploy/eso/externalsecrets/k-orc-clouds-yaml.yaml contains only the orc-system ExternalSecret (the openstack-namespace document is removed)
deploy/eso/kustomization.yaml line 18 still references k-orc-clouds-yaml.yaml (it still ships the orc-system document)
kubectl apply -k deploy/eso/ applies with no duplicate-owner conflict against the operator-created openstack ExternalSecret
The header comment no longer references issue #412 as a future supersession; it documents the operator-owned / static-orc-system split

stories.title: Maintainer reads docs that reflect the operator-owned seed
stories.want: the prose describing the deploy-infra-seeded bootstrap clouds.yaml, the CONTROLPLANE_NAME/KORC_KEYSTONE_AUTH_URL lockstep, and the static k-orc-clouds-yaml ExternalSecret updated
stories.so_that: the docs match the operator-owned per-CR ExternalSecret reality and the manual force-sync workaround is removed
criteria: controlplane-reconciler.md no longer attributes the bootstrap clouds.yaml to write-bootstrap-secrets.sh; it describes the operator-owned seed
quick-start-controlplane.md removes the 'keep the name controlplane / CONTROLPLANE_NAME lockstep' instruction and the WaitingForCloudsYaml force-sync workaround
controlplane-crd.md and infrastructure-manifests.md describe the operator-created per-CR ExternalSecret and the retained static orc-system copy
No doc still references KORC_KEYSTONE_AUTH_URL or describes the openstack-namespace static ExternalSecret as required

requirements.description: The operator SHALL seed a password-based clouds.yaml into the {cp.Name}-admin-app-credential Secret's clouds.yaml key during reconcileKORC, writing only when that key is empty (write-if-empty).
requirements.rationale: Breaks the AdminCredentialReady chicken-and-egg deadlock on a fresh cluster while never overwriting the minted credential-based clouds.yaml (boundary 9, CC-0114).

scenarios.name: Empty key is seeded
scenarios.when: seedBootstrapCloudsYAML runs and Secret.Data[appCredCloudsYAMLKey] is absent or empty
scenarios.then: the key is set to []byte(buildPasswordCloudsYAML(cp, password)) and the 'value' key is left untouched
and_then: the Secret keeps its controller owner reference to the ControlPlane

scenarios.name: Non-empty (minted) key is preserved
scenarios.when: seedBootstrapCloudsYAML runs and Secret.Data[appCredCloudsYAMLKey] already holds a credential-based clouds.yaml
scenarios.then: the existing bytes are left unchanged
and_then: controllerutil.CreateOrUpdate performs no Update (no churn)

scenarios.name: Re-mint repopulation
scenarios.when: a prior pass ran regenerateAppCredentialSecretValue which deleted the clouds.yaml key
scenarios.then: the next reconcileKORC pass re-seeds the password-based clouds.yaml
and_then: the re-authentication gap is bridged until reconcileAdminCredential assembles the new credential-based clouds.yaml

requirements.description: The operator SHALL create (create-or-update) an esov1.ExternalSecret named cp.Spec.KORC.AdminCredential.CloudCredentialsRef.SecretName (default k-orc-clouds-yaml) in childNamespace(cp), owner-referenced to the ControlPlane.
requirements.rationale: Replaces the static single-identity ExternalSecret with operator-owned per-CR templating that resolves to the correct OpenBao path for any CR name (boundary 6).

scenarios.name: ExternalSecret shape
scenarios.when: reconcileKORC runs ensureKORCCloudsYAMLExternalSecret
scenarios.then: an esov1.ExternalSecret exists in childNamespace(cp) with secretStoreRef {Kind: ClusterSecretStore, Name: openBaoClusterStoreName}, target {Name: <same name>, CreationPolicy: Owner}, and one data entry {SecretKey: clouds.yaml, RemoteRef: {Key: adminAppCredentialRemoteKeyFor(cp), Property: clouds.yaml}}
and_then: the object carries a controller owner reference to the ControlPlane (OwnerReferences len 1, Kind ControlPlane)

scenarios.name: Per-CR remoteRef key for non-default name
scenarios.when: the ControlPlane is named 'controlplane-a' in namespace 'tenant-a'
scenarios.then: the data entry RemoteRef.Key == openstack/keystone/tenant-a/controlplane-a/admin/app-credential
and_then: no manifest edit is required for the non-default name

scenarios.name: Idempotent re-create
scenarios.when: reconcileKORC runs twice with no spec change
scenarios.then: the second pass does not change the ExternalSecret's ResourceVersion
and_then: controllerutil.CreateOrUpdate treats the unchanged spec as a no-op

requirements.description: The operator SHALL ensure the {cp.Name}-admin-app-credential-backup PushSecret exists during reconcileKORC (before minting), reusing secrets.EnsurePushSecret with adminAppCredentialPushSecret(cp).
requirements.rationale: Populates the OpenBao path before the credential exists so ESO can materialize the ExternalSecret; the later EnsurePushSecret call in reconcileAdminCredential becomes a harmless no-op (boundary 3).

scenarios.name: PushSecret created early
scenarios.when: reconcileKORC runs on a fresh cluster with the admin password present
scenarios.then: the {cp.Name}-admin-app-credential-backup PushSecret is created with SecretStoreRefs[0].Name == openBaoClusterStoreName and Data[0].Match.RemoteRef.RemoteKey == adminAppCredentialRemoteKeyFor(cp)
and_then: the PushSecret is owner-referenced to the ControlPlane (EnsurePushSecret sets it)

scenarios.name: Later call is a no-op
scenarios.when: reconcileAdminCredential later calls EnsurePushSecret with the same desired spec
scenarios.then: the DeepEqual guard inside EnsurePushSecret performs no Update
and_then: ESO is not woken to re-push an unchanged credential

scenarios.name: Missing admin password defers
scenarios.when: the admin password Secret/key is not yet available
scenarios.then: reconcileKORC returns KORCReady=False (WaitingForAdminPassword) and requeues before reaching the seed steps
and_then: no PushSecret or ExternalSecret is created until the password exists

requirements.description: The seeded clouds.yaml SHALL contain endpoint_type: internal (key endpoint_type, not interface) and auth_url http://{cp.Name}-keystone.{cp.Namespace}.svc:5000/v3.
requirements.rationale: K-ORC runs in-cluster and only honours endpoint_type; an interface key or external auth_url makes every catalog call fail silently (boundary 2).

scenarios.name: Parsed endpoint_type
scenarios.when: the rendered clouds.yaml is parsed with a YAML unmarshaller
scenarios.then: clouds.<cloudName>.endpoint_type == 'internal'
and_then: no 'interface' key is present

scenarios.name: Parsed auth_url for default CR
scenarios.when: the ControlPlane is named 'controlplane' in namespace 'openstack'
scenarios.then: clouds.<cloudName>.auth.auth_url == http://controlplane-keystone.openstack.svc:5000/v3
and_then: the value equals keystoneEndpointURL(cp)

scenarios.name: Parsed auth_url for non-default CR
scenarios.when: the ControlPlane is named 'cp' in namespace 'default'
scenarios.then: clouds.<cloudName>.auth.auth_url == http://cp-keystone.default.svc:5000/v3
and_then: the projected name tracks cp.Name, not a hardcoded value

requirements.description: The seed step SHALL NOT trigger a re-mint or overwrite the 'value' key, and a steady-state reconcile after the credential is minted SHALL leave the credential-based clouds.yaml intact.
requirements.rationale: Mirrors the existing 'write if missing' discipline; the minted credential is the source of truth once present (boundary 9).

scenarios.name: Value key preserved
scenarios.when: seedBootstrapCloudsYAML runs on a Secret that already has a 'value' key
scenarios.then: the 'value' bytes are unchanged
and_then: only the clouds.yaml key may be written, and only when empty

scenarios.name: Minted document survives steady-state
scenarios.when: reconcileKORC runs after reconcileAdminCredential has written buildAppCredCloudsYAML output to clouds.yaml
scenarios.then: the clouds.yaml bytes still equal the credential-based document (containing application_credential_id)
and_then: no Update to the Secret occurs from the seed

scenarios.name: No spurious re-mint
scenarios.when: the admin password hash annotation matches
scenarios.then: the seed does not delete the clouds.yaml key or alter the AC hash
and_then: the existing re-mint trigger (hash mismatch) remains the only re-mint signal

requirements.description: The KORC_KEYSTONE_AUTH_URL knob SHALL be fully removed from hack/deploy-infra.sh and deploy/openbao/bootstrap/write-bootstrap-secrets.sh.
requirements.rationale: Its only consumer was the shell seed being removed; keeping a dead knob invites drift (boundary 5).

scenarios.name: No repo matches
scenarios.when: grep -rn KORC_KEYSTONE_AUTH_URL is run over hack/ and deploy/openbao/bootstrap/
scenarios.then: no matches are returned
and_then: the export line in deploy-infra.sh is deleted and the variable reference in write-bootstrap-secrets.sh is deleted

scenarios.name: Deploy-infra still succeeds
scenarios.when: make deploy-infra WITH_CONTROLPLANE=true runs with no KORC_KEYSTONE_AUTH_URL set
scenarios.then: it succeeds
and_then: KORC_CONTROLPLANES is still exported for the Model B admin-password loop

scenarios.name: WITH_CONTROLPLANE_CR guidance updated
scenarios.when: a reader views the WITH_CONTROLPLANE_CR branch guidance
scenarios.then: it no longer instructs keeping CONTROLPLANE_NAME in lockstep with a K-ORC seed
and_then: it may still mention CONTROLPLANE_NAME for the Model B path and bundled-CR rename

requirements.description: The shell K-ORC clouds.yaml seed SHALL be removed (function, call site, ac_path local, and the ac_path mark_eso_managed) while the Model B admin-password seed and the KORC_CONTROLPLANES loop are retained.
requirements.rationale: Centralizes the seed in the operator; the Model B admin-password mechanism is a separate concern that must keep working (Non-Goals).

scenarios.name: Seed function gone
scenarios.when: write-bootstrap-secrets.sh is inspected
scenarios.then: seed_korc_bootstrap_clouds_yaml() and its call inside the KORC_CONTROLPLANES loop are absent
and_then: the ac_path local and its mark_eso_managed "${ac_path}" call are absent

scenarios.name: Model B retained
scenarios.when: the KORC_CONTROLPLANES loop is inspected
scenarios.then: write_secret_if_missing on kv-v2/bootstrap/{ns}/{keystone}/admin and its mark_eso_managed "${admin_path}" remain
and_then: the KORC_CONTROLPLANES header comment no longer lists the app-credential clouds.yaml seed

scenarios.name: Script still parses
scenarios.when: bash -n write-bootstrap-secrets.sh is run
scenarios.then: it reports no syntax error
and_then: the shellcheck-targeted disables tied only to the removed seed are also removed

requirements.description: The static deploy/eso/externalsecrets/k-orc-clouds-yaml.yaml SHALL contain only the orc-system ExternalSecret; the openstack-namespace document SHALL be removed.
requirements.rationale: The operator now owns the childNamespace copy; two controllers must never own the same object (boundary 6, server-side-apply field ownership).

scenarios.name: Only orc-system remains
scenarios.when: the manifest is parsed
scenarios.then: exactly one ExternalSecret document exists with metadata.namespace orc-system
and_then: no document with metadata.namespace openstack remains

scenarios.name: Kustomization intact
scenarios.when: deploy/eso/kustomization.yaml is inspected
scenarios.then: k-orc-clouds-yaml.yaml is still in the resources list
and_then: kubectl apply -k deploy/eso/ applies cleanly with no duplicate-owner conflict

scenarios.name: Header rewritten
scenarios.when: the manifest header comment is read
scenarios.then: it describes the operator-owned per-CR ExternalSecret + the retained static orc-system copy and drops the 'issue #412 supersedes it' language
and_then: the orc-system document keeps the default-identity remoteRef.key

requirements.description: The unit test TestReconcileAdminCredential_FreshClusterWithoutCloudsYamlSeedDoesNotPush SHALL be reworked (or replaced) to assert the operator now seeds and the chain converges.
requirements.rationale: That test currently asserts the deadlock holds; the operator-owned seed deliberately breaks it (boundary 4).

scenarios.name: Operator seeds and pushes
scenarios.when: reconcileKORC runs on a fresh cluster (admin password present, no pre-existing clouds.yaml ExternalSecret)
scenarios.then: the {cp.Name}-admin-app-credential-backup PushSecret IS created and the {cp.Name}-admin-app-credential Secret's clouds.yaml key holds the password-based document
and_then: the per-CR ExternalSecret is created in childNamespace(cp)

scenarios.name: Old assertion removed
scenarios.when: the reworked test body is inspected
scenarios.then: the 'no PushSecret may be created while the clouds.yaml ExternalSecret is unseeded' assertion is gone
and_then: the test name reflects the new behaviour (e.g. operator seeds on a fresh cluster)

requirements.description: The integration test (envtest) SHALL be reworked to assert the operator creates the k-orc-clouds-yaml ExternalSecret rather than pre-creating it, then SimulateExternalSecretSync against the operator-created object.
requirements.rationale: The integration tests previously simulated the shell seed by pre-creating the ExternalSecret; the operator now owns it (boundary 6).

scenarios.name: Full-reconcile test
scenarios.when: TestIntegration_FullReconcile_ManagedToReady runs
scenarios.then: it waits (g.Eventually) for the operator-created ExternalSecret in the CR namespace instead of c.Create-ing it, then SimulateExternalSecretSync against it
and_then: AdminCredentialReady=True is still reached

scenarios.name: Multi-CP test
scenarios.when: TestIntegration_MultiControlPlane_DistinctAdminCredentialPaths runs
scenarios.then: its drive helper waits for each per-CR operator-created ExternalSecret before simulating sync
and_then: the distinct admin-credential OpenBao paths assertion still holds

requirements.description: The change SHALL introduce no new RBAC group/resource, CRD field, sub-reconciler, or status condition.
requirements.rationale: external-secrets;pushsecrets RBAC and the KORC/AdminCredential condition wiring already exist; no *_types.go change means no make manifests/generate (boundary 8, Non-Goals).

scenarios.name: RBAC unchanged
scenarios.when: controlplane_controller.go markers and _helpers.tpl are compared before/after
scenarios.then: the external-secrets.io externalsecrets;pushsecrets verbs are unchanged
and_then: no new +kubebuilder:rbac marker is added

scenarios.name: Condition map unchanged
scenarios.when: instrumentation.go subReconcilerConditionTypes is inspected
scenarios.then: no new entry is added and the drift-guard tests pass
and_then: the new steps live inside the existing KORC sub-reconciler

scenarios.name: No CRD drift
scenarios.when: the CRD bases and Helm CRD copies are compared
scenarios.then: they are byte-identical to before the change
and_then: no make manifests/generate is required

requirements.description: The end-to-end full-controlplane-keystone suite SHALL continue to pass and the multi-controlplane fixtures SHALL be runnable without per-identity seed overrides once cluster prerequisites are present.
requirements.rationale: The named acceptance gate for the issue; the default path key is unchanged and non-default names now resolve via the operator (Motivation).

scenarios.name: Default-name e2e passes
scenarios.when: the full-controlplane-keystone Chainsaw suite runs on a fresh kind cluster with WITH_CONTROLPLANE=true
scenarios.then: AdminCredentialReady=True is reached and the orc-system ExternalSecret still materializes
and_then: no comment in the e2e files attributes the seed to write-bootstrap-secrets.sh

scenarios.name: Non-default-name materializes per-CR ExternalSecret
scenarios.when: a non-default ControlPlane (e.g. controlplane-a/tenant-a) reconciles with its prerequisites present
scenarios.then: the operator-created per-CR ExternalSecret materializes and AdminCredentialReady=True is reached
and_then: no per-identity KORC_KEYSTONE_AUTH_URL or remoteRef.key edit is required

tasks.title: Add esov1 import and ensureKORCCloudsYAMLExternalSecret(ctx, cp) to reconcile_korc.go: inline controllerutil.CreateOrUpdate of an esov1.ExternalSecret in childNamespace(cp) named CloudCredentialsRef.SecretName (fallback korcCloudsYamlSecretName), SecretStoreRef{ClusterSecretStore, openBaoClusterStoreName}, Target{Name, CreatePolicyOwner}, Data[{SecretKey: appCredCloudsYAMLKey, RemoteRef{Key: adminAppCredentialRemoteKeyFor(cp), Property: appCredCloudsYAMLKey}}], SetControllerReference last. Add unit tests TestEnsureKORCCloudsYAMLExternalSecret_ShapeAndOwnerRef, _PerCRRemoteKeyForNonDefaultName, _IdempotentNoChurn (REQ-002).

Add seedBootstrapCloudsYAML(ctx, cp, password) to reconcile_korc.go: controllerutil.CreateOrUpdate on the {cp.Name}-admin-app-credential Secret, write []byte(buildPasswordCloudsYAML(cp, password)) into appCredCloudsYAMLKey ONLY when empty, never touch the value key, SetControllerReference last. Add unit tests TestSeedBootstrapCloudsYAML_WritesWhenCloudsYamlKeyEmpty and _DoesNotOverwriteMintedCloudsYaml (REQ-001, REQ-005).

Add unit test TestSeedBootstrapCloudsYAML_RenderedDocumentParsesWithInternalEndpointAndProjectedAuthURL using sigs.k8s.io/yaml to Unmarshal the seeded clouds.yaml and assert clouds.<cloud>.endpoint_type == 'internal' and auth.auth_url == http://{cp.Name}-keystone.{cp.Namespace}.svc:5000/v3 (parse the rendered document, not the input) (REQ-004).

Wire the three steps into reconcileKORC immediately AFTER ensureAppCredentialSecret (reconcile_korc.go:410) and BEFORE the re-mint/CreateOrUpdate AC decision (line 428): (1) seedBootstrapCloudsYAML(ctx, cp, password); (2) secrets.EnsurePushSecret(ctx, r.Client, r.Scheme, cp, adminAppCredentialPushSecret(cp)); (3) ensureKORCCloudsYAMLExternalSecret(ctx, cp); each on failure sets KORCReady=False with a distinct Reason and returns. Leave a DECISION comment explaining placement (see implementation_notes) (REQ-001, REQ-002, REQ-003).

Rework the deadlock test: rename/replace TestReconcileAdminCredential_FreshClusterWithoutCloudsYamlSeedDoesNotPush to TestReconcileKORC_FreshClusterSeedsCloudsYamlAndCreatesPushSecretAndExternalSecret driving reconcileKORC (cp + adminPasswordSecret(), no pre-existing ExternalSecret); assert PushSecret + per-CR ExternalSecret created and password clouds.yaml seeded; remove the 'no PushSecret may be created' assertion. Add TestReconcileKORC_PushSecretRemoteKeyMatchesPerCRPath (REQ-003, REQ-009).

Add edge-case unit tests: TestReconcileKORC_DefersBeforeSeedWhenAdminPasswordMissing (no admin password -> WaitingForAdminPassword, no PushSecret/ExternalSecret created) and TestReconcileKORC_SteadyStateDoesNotOverwriteMintedCloudsYaml (pre-existing minted clouds.yaml survives a reconcileKORC pass) (REQ-003, REQ-005).

Rework integration_test.go TestIntegration_FullReconcile_ManagedToReady (lines ~298-315): delete the c.Create of cloudsYamlES; instead g.Eventually-Get the operator-created k-orc-clouds-yaml ExternalSecret in ns.Name (assert remoteRef.key == adminAppCredentialRemoteKeyFor(cp), secretKey/property == clouds.yaml, controller ownerRef), then SimulateExternalSecretSync against it. Update the stale 'seeded by write-bootstrap-secrets.sh' comment (REQ-010).

Rework the multi-CP drive helper in integration_test.go (lines ~478-488) the same way: wait for each per-CR operator-created ExternalSecret before SimulateExternalSecretSync; confirm TestIntegration_MultiControlPlane_DistinctAdminCredentialPaths still asserts keyA != keyB. Leave the mint-credential split lock (lines ~415-426) unchanged (REQ-010, REQ-002).

Edit deploy/openbao/bootstrap/write-bootstrap-secrets.sh: delete seed_korc_bootstrap_clouds_yaml() and its doc comment (lines ~121-218), its call + comment in the KORC_CONTROLPLANES loop (lines ~268-278), the ac_path local (line ~255), and the mark_eso_managed "${ac_path}" (line ~278). Keep the Model B admin-password seed (lines ~259-266) and its mark_eso_managed "${admin_path}". Update the KORC_CONTROLPLANES header comment (lines ~23-34) to drop the app-credential clouds.yaml seed bullet. Verify with bash -n (REQ-007).

Edit hack/deploy-infra.sh: delete the KORC_KEYSTONE_AUTH_URL export (line ~1200); keep the KORC_CONTROLPLANES export (line ~1199). Update the comment block (lines ~1170-1197), the parenthetical (lines ~1188-1190), the CONTROLPLANE_NAME context (lines ~95-102), and the WITH_CONTROLPLANE_CR guidance (lines ~1320-1321) to stop describing the K-ORC seed lockstep. Verify grep -rn KORC_KEYSTONE_AUTH_URL over hack/ + deploy/openbao/bootstrap/ returns nothing; bash -n passes (REQ-006).

Edit deploy/eso/externalsecrets/k-orc-clouds-yaml.yaml: remove the openstack-namespace ExternalSecret document (lines ~44-65); keep the orc-system document (lines ~66-85) with its default-identity remoteRef.key. Rewrite the header comment (lines ~5-43) to delete the 'issue #412 supersedes it' shim language and describe the operator-owned per-CR (childNamespace) ExternalSecret + the retained static orc-system copy. Leave deploy/eso/kustomization.yaml line 18 in place (REQ-008).

Update docs/reference/c5c3/controlplane-reconciler.md: the clouds.yaml gate section (lines ~518-525) to attribute the bootstrap clouds.yaml to the operator's reconcileKORC seed (seedBootstrapCloudsYAML) instead of write-bootstrap-secrets.sh; update the data-flow ASCII (lines ~652-653) and the round-trip reference (line ~1010) to show the operator-owned per-CR ExternalSecret. Reference CC-0114 (REQ-010).

Update docs/quick-start-controlplane.md: remove the 'keep the name controlplane / CONTROLPLANE_NAME lockstep' instruction (lines ~53-55), update the inline sample comment about the seeded Secret (line ~94), and remove the WaitingForCloudsYaml force-sync workaround (lines ~121-127) since the operator now seeds. Reference CC-0114 (REQ-010).

Update docs/reference/c5c3/controlplane-crd.md: the CloudCredentialsRef.secretName field doc (line ~322), defaulting section (lines ~647-653), and the WaitingForCloudsYaml condition row (lines ~739-740) to describe the operator-created per-CR ExternalSecret. Update docs/reference/infrastructure/infrastructure-manifests.md Admin Credential Chain / ESO ExternalSecrets section (lines ~676-700), the two-namespace table (lines ~682-685), and the K-ORC notes (lines ~448-453) to describe operator-owned childNamespace ExternalSecret + static orc-system copy; delete the #412 follow-up wording. Reference CC-0114 (REQ-010).

Cross-check the remaining infra docs for stale seed prose: docs/reference/infrastructure/openbao-bootstrap.md (confirm only the Model B admin-password row at line ~325 remains and no app-credential clouds.yaml seed is described) and docs/reference/infrastructure/e2e-deployment.md; update any sentence that still attributes the K-ORC clouds.yaml to the shell seed or KORC_KEYSTONE_AUTH_URL. Reference CC-0114 (REQ-010).

E2E verification (full-controlplane-keystone): confirm the default-name Chainsaw suite still reaches AdminCredentialReady=True with the operator-owned seed (path key unchanged) and update any comment that attributes the seed to write-bootstrap-secrets.sh. Document that execution requires a kind cluster (not runnable in the sandbox) and note the result. Reference CC-0114 (REQ-012).

E2E verification (multi-controlplane): with tenant-a/tenant-b prerequisites present, verify each per-CR operator-created ExternalSecret materializes and AdminCredentialReady=True is reached without per-identity overrides; confirm the presence guard still SKIPs cleanly when the stack is absent. Note that wiring the tenant prerequisites into kind CI remains a documented fast-follow (deferred). Reference CC-0114 (REQ-012).

test_specifications.story: The operator never clobbers a minted credential-based clouds.yaml
test_specifications.expected: [unit] Given a {cp.Name}-admin-app-credential Secret with only the 'value' key (mintedAppCredSecret(cp)), seedBootstrapCloudsYAML sets clouds.yaml to buildPasswordCloudsYAML(cp, password) (contains username/password, not application_credential_id) and leaves 'value' untouched; CC-0114 REQ-001

test_specifications.story: The operator never clobbers a minted credential-based clouds.yaml
test_specifications.expected: [unit] Given a Secret whose clouds.yaml holds buildAppCredCloudsYAML(cp, 'ac-id', 'secret') (contains application_credential_id), seedBootstrapCloudsYAML leaves the bytes byte-for-byte unchanged; CC-0114 REQ-001, REQ-005

test_specifications.story: The seeded clouds.yaml authenticates K-ORC in-cluster
test_specifications.expected: [unit] sigs.k8s.io/yaml.Unmarshal of the seeded clouds.yaml yields clouds.admin.endpoint_type == 'internal' and clouds.admin.auth.auth_url == http://controlplane-keystone.openstack.svc:5000/v3 for a default CR (and http://cp-keystone.default.svc:5000/v3 for korcControlPlane()); CC-0114 REQ-004

test_specifications.story: The c5c3-operator self-seeds the bootstrap clouds.yaml and per-CR ExternalSecret
test_specifications.expected: [unit] After ensureKORCCloudsYAMLExternalSecret, an esov1.ExternalSecret in childNamespace(cp) named CloudCredentialsRef.SecretName has SecretStoreRef{Kind:ClusterSecretStore,Name:openBaoClusterStoreName}, Target{Name:<name>,CreationPolicy:CreatePolicyOwner}, Data[0]{SecretKey:'clouds.yaml',RemoteRef{Key:adminAppCredentialRemoteKeyFor(cp),Property:'clouds.yaml'}}, and a controller OwnerReference (len 1, Kind ControlPlane); CC-0114 REQ-002

test_specifications.story: Platform operator brings up an arbitrarily-named ControlPlane on the default deploy path
test_specifications.expected: [unit] For a ControlPlane named 'controlplane-a' in 'tenant-a', the created ExternalSecret Data[0].RemoteRef.Key == 'openstack/keystone/tenant-a/controlplane-a/admin/app-credential'; CC-0114 REQ-002

test_specifications.story: The c5c3-operator self-seeds the bootstrap clouds.yaml and per-CR ExternalSecret
test_specifications.expected: [unit] Running ensureKORCCloudsYAMLExternalSecret twice leaves the ExternalSecret ResourceVersion unchanged on the second pass (capture-RV-then-reconcile idiom, mirroring TestReconcileCatalog_Idempotent); CC-0114 REQ-002

test_specifications.story: The c5c3-operator self-seeds the bootstrap clouds.yaml and per-CR ExternalSecret
test_specifications.expected: [unit] Reworked from TestReconcileAdminCredential_FreshClusterWithoutCloudsYamlSeedDoesNotPush: running reconcileKORC with cp + adminPasswordSecret() (no pre-existing ExternalSecret) creates the {cp.Name}-admin-app-credential-backup PushSecret, seeds the password clouds.yaml, and creates the per-CR ExternalSecret; the old 'no PushSecret' assertion is gone; CC-0114 REQ-003, REQ-009

test_specifications.story: The c5c3-operator self-seeds the bootstrap clouds.yaml and per-CR ExternalSecret
test_specifications.expected: [unit] After reconcileKORC the created PushSecret has SecretStoreRefs[0].Name == openBaoClusterStoreName and Data[0].Match.RemoteRef.RemoteKey == adminAppCredentialRemoteKeyFor(cp); CC-0114 REQ-003

test_specifications.story: The operator never clobbers a minted credential-based clouds.yaml
test_specifications.expected: [unit] Edge case: reconcileKORC with no admin password Secret returns KORCReady=False (WaitingForAdminPassword) and requeues, and neither the PushSecret nor the ExternalSecret is created; CC-0114 REQ-003

test_specifications.story: The operator never clobbers a minted credential-based clouds.yaml
test_specifications.expected: [unit] Edge case: with a Secret whose clouds.yaml already holds the credential-based document, a reconcileKORC pass leaves clouds.yaml unchanged (still contains application_credential_id) and does not bump the Secret ResourceVersion via the seed; CC-0114 REQ-005

test_specifications.story: Maintainer reads docs that reflect the operator-owned seed
test_specifications.expected: [integration] Reworked: do NOT c.Create the k-orc-clouds-yaml ExternalSecret; g.Eventually-Get the operator-created ExternalSecret in ns.Name, assert its remoteRef.key/secretKey/property/ownerRef, then SimulateExternalSecretSync against it; AdminCredentialReady=True still reached; CC-0114 REQ-010

test_specifications.story: Platform operator brings up an arbitrarily-named ControlPlane on the default deploy path
test_specifications.expected: [integration] Reworked drive helper (lines ~478-488): wait for each per-CR operator-created ExternalSecret then SimulateExternalSecretSync; the distinct per-CR admin-credential OpenBao path assertion (keyA != keyB) still holds; CC-0114 REQ-010, REQ-002

test_specifications.story: The operator never clobbers a minted credential-based clouds.yaml
test_specifications.expected: [integration] After the mint, the {cp.Name}-admin-app-credential Secret's clouds.yaml holds the credential-based document (contains application_credential_id), proving the operator seed bridged then yielded to the minted credential; CC-0114 REQ-005

test_specifications.story: Maintainer reads docs that reflect the operator-owned seed
test_specifications.expected: [e2e] On a fresh kind cluster with WITH_CONTROLPLANE=true (no seed_korc_bootstrap_clouds_yaml), the link-by-link chain reaches AdminCredentialReady=True; the CP_NS and orc-system ExternalSecrets both materialize; no comment attributes the seed to write-bootstrap-secrets.sh; CC-0114 REQ-012

test_specifications.story: Platform operator brings up an arbitrarily-named ControlPlane on the default deploy path
test_specifications.expected: [e2e] With tenant-a/tenant-b prerequisites present, controlplane-a/controlplane-b each materialize their per-CR ExternalSecret (operator-created) and reach AdminCredentialReady=True without any per-identity seed override; the presence guard still SKIPs cleanly when the stack is absent; CC-0114 REQ-012

test_specifications.story: Deploy-infra maintainer no longer maintains the K-ORC auth_url lockstep
test_specifications.expected: [unit] grep -rn KORC_KEYSTONE_AUTH_URL over hack/ and deploy/openbao/bootstrap/ returns zero matches; bash -n write-bootstrap-secrets.sh and deploy-infra.sh report no syntax error; CC-0114 REQ-006, REQ-007

test_specifications.story: GitOps maintainer sees one operator-owned ExternalSecret and one static off-path copy
test_specifications.expected: [e2e] Parsing the manifest yields exactly one ExternalSecret with metadata.namespace orc-system and none with namespace openstack; kubectl apply -k deploy/eso/ applies cleanly; CC-0114 REQ-008

test_specifications.story: GitOps maintainer sees one operator-owned ExternalSecret and one static off-path copy
test_specifications.expected: [e2e] The header comment contains no 'issue #412 supersedes it' / shim language and describes the operator-owned per-CR ExternalSecret + the retained static orc-system copy; the orc-system document keeps the default-identity remoteRef.key; CC-0114 REQ-008

test_specifications.story: The operator never clobbers a minted credential-based clouds.yaml
test_specifications.expected: [unit] After regenerateAppCredentialSecretValue deletes the clouds.yaml key (simulated by a Secret with only 'value'), seedBootstrapCloudsYAML re-writes the password-based clouds.yaml — bridging the re-auth gap; CC-0114 REQ-001

test_specifications.story: The seeded clouds.yaml authenticates K-ORC in-cluster
test_specifications.expected: [unit] The parsed clouds.<cloud> map contains key 'endpoint_type' and NO key 'interface' (the K-ORC scope builder drops interface) — boundary 2; CC-0114 REQ-004

test_specifications.story: Deploy-infra maintainer no longer maintains the K-ORC auth_url lockstep

test_specifications.story: Deploy-infra maintainer no longer maintains the K-ORC auth_url lockstep

test_specifications.story: Deploy-infra maintainer no longer maintains the K-ORC auth_url lockstep
test_specifications.expected: [unit] bash -n write-bootstrap-secrets.sh reports no syntax error after deletion; shellcheck passes; the KORC_CONTROLPLANES header comment no longer lists the app-credential clouds.yaml seed; CC-0114 REQ-007

test_specifications.story: The c5c3-operator self-seeds the bootstrap clouds.yaml and per-CR ExternalSecret
test_specifications.expected: [unit] The existing condition-coverage drift guards still pass with no new entry in subReconcilerConditionTypes — proving the new steps live inside the existing KORC sub-reconciler and add no condition; CC-0114 REQ-011

test_specifications.test_file: CI / git-diff gate (make manifests, make generate)
test_specifications.story: The c5c3-operator self-seeds the bootstrap clouds.yaml and per-CR ExternalSecret
test_specifications.expected: [integration] git diff after make manifests && make generate shows no change under operators/c5c3/config/crd/bases, the Helm crds copy, or the +kubebuilder:rbac markers — no *_types.go change occurred; CC-0114 REQ-011

review_criteria: BLOCKER: reconcileKORC creates the per-CR esov1.ExternalSecret in childNamespace(cp) with remoteRef.key == adminAppCredentialRemoteKeyFor(cp), secretKey == 'clouds.yaml', property == 'clouds.yaml', Target.CreationPolicy == CreatePolicyOwner, and a controller owner reference — asserted by a passing unit test (REQ-002)

BLOCKER: seedBootstrapCloudsYAML is write-if-empty — it writes the password clouds.yaml only when the key is empty and never overwrites a minted credential-based clouds.yaml or the 'value' key — asserted by passing unit tests (REQ-001, REQ-005)
BLOCKER: the seeded clouds.yaml, when parsed as YAML, has endpoint_type: internal and auth_url http://{cp.Name}-keystone.{cp.Namespace}.svc:5000/v3 (REQ-004)

BLOCKER: grep -rn KORC_KEYSTONE_AUTH_URL over hack/ and deploy/openbao/bootstrap/ returns no matches; seed_korc_bootstrap_clouds_yaml and its call/ac_path/mark_eso_managed are deleted; Model B admin-password seed retained (REQ-006, REQ-007)

BLOCKER: deploy/eso/externalsecrets/k-orc-clouds-yaml.yaml contains only the orc-system ExternalSecret; kustomization line 18 retained; kubectl apply -k deploy/eso/ has no duplicate-owner conflict (REQ-008)

CRITICAL: the reworked deadlock test drives reconcileKORC and asserts the PushSecret + ExternalSecret are created and the password clouds.yaml is seeded; the old 'no push on a fresh cluster' assertion is gone (REQ-009)

CRITICAL: integration_test.go asserts the operator creates the k-orc-clouds-yaml ExternalSecret (no manual c.Create) then SimulateExternalSecretSync against it; both full-reconcile and multi-CP tests still reach AdminCredentialReady=True (REQ-010)

CRITICAL: no new RBAC marker, Helm ClusterRole change, CRD field, sub-reconciler, status condition, or instrumentation.go map entry — go test ./operators/c5c3/... passes including the condition/CRD drift guards, and no make manifests/generate diff is produced (REQ-011)

CRITICAL: the three new steps run after ensureAppCredentialSecret and before the re-mint/CreateOrUpdate AC decision, each guarded with a distinct KORCReady=False Reason on failure; the esov1 import is added and the change is surgical (imports the v1 API, not a second v1alpha1) (REQ-001, REQ-002, REQ-003)

MINOR: go vet / golangci-lint / gofumpt clean (use the pinned tool versions); SPDX headers unchanged; docs reference Feature ID CC-0114 and describe the operator-owned per-CR ExternalSecret with the retained static orc-system copy (REQ-010)
MINOR: reconcileKORC remains idempotent (no Secret/PushSecret/ExternalSecret churn on steady-state passes — ResourceVersion stable), matching the existing no-churn discipline
